title: Payton Pritchard’s huge night leads short-handed Celtics over streaking Hawks: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Boston Celtics' Payton Pritchard scored 36 points, leading the team to a 109-102 win against the Atlanta Hawks.
Jayson Tatum contributed 25 points, 12 rebounds, and five assists despite a challenging shooting night.
Jaylen Brown is listed as day-to-day due to Achilles tendinitis, affecting his availability for upcoming games.

title: Why did Jayson Tatum come back this season? The Celtics’ success had ‘a lot’ to do with it.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics ten months post-Achilles' surgery, prioritizing team contention over lengthy rehab.
The Celtics are second in the standings, with Tatum aiming to strengthen a playoff-bound team despite shooting struggles.
Tatum averages 9.2 rebounds per game since return, with versatile play including occasional small-ball center roles.

title: Police: Man killed in Lowell apartment complex shooting
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A man was killed following a shooting after an altercation at a Lowell apartment complex on Pawtucket Boulevard.
The incident involved two armed men who allegedly knew each other and occurred just before 4 p.m. on Friday.
The shooting is under investigation by the Middlesex DA's Office, Massachusetts State Police, and Lowell Police Department.

title: Report: Connecticut Sun to relocate to Houston with purchase by Rockets owners
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Connecticut Sun will relocate to Houston in 2027, renamed as the Houston Comets.
The Houston Rockets' owners, the Fertitta family, are purchasing the Sun for a WNBA-record $300 million.
Former Celtics minority owner Steve Pagliuca's $325 million bid to move the Sun to Boston was barred by the WNBA.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'From the World of John Wick: Ballerina' starring Ana de Armas is now streaming on HBO Max.
'Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice', a Vince Vaughn time loop comedy, is available on Hulu.
'Daredevil: Born Again' Season 2 featuring Charlie Cox is now streaming on Disney+.

title: Celtics’ Jaylen Brown (left Achilles tendinitis) out vs. Hawks
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown ruled out for the Celtics' game against the Hawks due to left Achilles tendinitis.
Coach Joe Mazzulla stated Brown's condition is 'day-to-day' following his recent 31-point game performance.
Celtics hold a 48-24 record, second in the Eastern Conference, despite key player injuries this season.

title: ‘Where does it end?’: Readers react to gas-powered leaf blower bans
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Arlington and Lexington, Massachusetts, have banned gas-powered leaf blowers to address noise and emission concerns.
A Boston.com poll revealed 73% of 250 respondents oppose the ban, citing practicality issues for larger yards.
Supporters of the ban highlight noise, fumes, and health concerns, advocating for quieter, cleaner alternatives.

title: What to know about the highly mutated new COVID strain found in Mass. wastewater
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The BA.3.2 COVID variant, called the 'cicada' variant, has been detected in Massachusetts wastewater.
BA.3.2 was first found in South Africa in November 2024 and has spread to 23 countries.
Current COVID vaccines are expected to protect against severe disease despite BA.3.2's mutations.

title: DCF workers who authorized removal of kids in Waltham could stand trial, judge rules
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A federal judge ruled a lawsuit can proceed against two DCF employees over the removal of a couple's children.
The parents, who were cleared of abuse, allege their children were seized without a warrant or just cause.
Judge Saris found a "genuine dispute" on whether the couple consented to the removal under coercion in 2022.

title: 3 New England restaurants among the most anticipated openings, according to Food & Wine
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Willie's on Beacon Hill by Jamie Bissonnette now open, offering pizzas, pastas, and global appetizers.
Ladyfish in Portland by Jordan Rubin opens May 1, featuring Maine seafood with Chinese and Italian influences.
La Tavernetta in East Boston by Douglass Williams opening this spring, will serve oysters and spritzes.
